name: Numbers Drill — Doses, Vitals, Dates & Spelling
_note: Skill drill. Standard 4 metric keys re-pitched: medical_accuracy = correctness of the value spoken, clinical_reasoning slot = correct spoken conventions, communication_fluency = clarity of digit/letter pronunciation (the drill's focus).

anchors.7-8: Occasional minor errors; meaning always clear

anchors.9-10: Every number, dose, date, and spelling is stated correctly
Natural conventions: 'one-forty over ninety', '500 milligrams twice daily', dates and units said the way clinicians do
anchors.5-6: Understandable but uses non-standard or literal forms
anchors.1-2: Conventions wrong enough to be unsafe

anchors.9-10: Digits and letters are crisp and unambiguous
Exceptional empathy and professionalism; creates psychological safety
anchors.7-8: Good empathy; acknowledges emotion; polite and professional
anchors.5-6: Adequate but mechanical empathy; some emotional cues missed
anchors.3-4: Little empathic response; information delivery prioritized
anchors.1-2: No empathic response; dismissive or unprofessional

checklist.item: stated blood pressure in natural spoken form (e.g. 'one-forty over ninety')
stated a dose with both amount and unit (e.g. '500 milligrams')
stated a frequency clearly (e.g. 'twice a day')
said a date in a clear, natural way
spelled a name clearly, letter by letter
self-corrected or repeated when something was unclear
